fields.title: The Master of Mankind
fields.synopsis: While Horus' rebellion tears across the galaxy, a war of a different kind rages beneath Terra itself. The Ten Thousand Custodian Guard, joined by the Sisters of Silence and forces of the Mechanicum, battle daemonic entities infesting the ancient Eldar webway—consequences of Magnus the Red's catastrophic intrusion. As traitor Space Marines and corrupted Titans add their strength to Chaos, the defenses of the Imperial Palace crumble. Only the Emperor can stand against the tide, but salvation demands terrible sacrifices. Through the eyes of loyal warriors and forgotten heroes, the story explores the true cost of the Emperor's grand vision and his uncompromising will to preserve mankind's future, even as hope itself begins to die.

fields.title: Garro
fields.synopsis: Nathaniel Garro, a former Battle-Captain of the Death Guard, emerges from the shadows after escaping the treachery of the Heresy. Stripped of his legion colours and transformed into a grey-armoured knight-errant, he serves as Malcador the Sigillite's agent in a secret war against the traitors. Across distant battlefields from Calth to Terra itself, Garro undertakes a sacred mission to recruit loyalist warriors from fallen legions—warriors caught between light and darkness. As he gathers these broken heroes, Garro's own faith begins to kindle, leading him to question his place in an Imperium that knows nothing of the spiritual awakening taking root in its shadows. His journey of purpose becomes a journey of conscience, as he searches for redemption and meaning amid the ruins of the Emperor's greatest dream.

fields.title: Shattered Legions
fields.synopsis: Following the devastating massacre at Isstvan V, the survivors of three Space Marine Legions—the Iron Hands, Raven Guard, and Salamanders—band together to form a guerrilla force dedicated to harassing the traitor advance toward Terra. This collection of ten interconnected short stories and novellas chronicles their desperate struggle for survival and vengeance. Led by the resourceful warlord Shadrak Meduson, these shattered warriors undertake a relentless campaign of hit-and-run warfare across the galaxy, adapting their tactics and learning to fight as a unified force despite their losses. The tales explore the physical and emotional toll of betrayal, the weight of leadership in hopelessness, and the determination of loyalist Space Marines to resist overwhelming odds and exact retribution against those who betrayed them.

fields.title: The Crimson King
fields.synopsis: After the Space Wolves razed Prospero, Magnus the Red retreated with the Thousand Sons to a desolate realm within the Eye of Terror. Isolated from the broader Heresy, he dedicates himself to preserving Tizca's lost knowledge while his fractured mind deteriorates, his consciousness splintering across the warp. Recognizing their primarch's decline, Ahriman and his fellow sorcerer-warriors embark on a perilous quest to reassemble Magnus's scattered essence. Leading a hand-picked band that includes the hedonistic Lucius, Ahriman ventures into the warp's depths, confronting both daemons and the moral compromises that will ultimately define the legion's path to damnation. The journey explores themes of paternal obligation, sacrificial desperation, and the terrible price of hubris as the Thousand Sons discover that salvation and damnation are often indistinguishable.

fields.title: Tallarn
fields.synopsis: The verdant world of Tallarn was once a prosperous logistics hub for the Imperium's Great Crusade forces. When the Iron Warriors under Primarch Perturabo launch a devastating virus-bombing campaign, the world is transformed into a radioactive wasteland. Yet the attack proves only the beginning. Entrenched survivors and reinforcing Imperial forces wage a brutal, prolonged campaign across Tallarn's poisoned surface and underground fortifications, engaging in massive armoured battles that become the greatest tank warfare of the entire Heresy. This sprawling narrative, told through multiple perspectives and timeframes, explores the desperation of soldiers fighting for survival, the hidden machinations of shadowy operatives on both sides, and Perturabo's darker purpose for seizing the world—revealing fractures within the traitor alliance itself as Chaos forces work at cross-purposes while the Imperium struggles to hold the line.

fields.title: Ruinstorm
fields.synopsis: In the aftermath of the Imperium Secundus revelation, three loyalist primarchs—Sanguinius, Roboute Guilliman, and the Lion El'Jonson—must lead their fleets through a galaxy-spanning warp anomaly to reach Terra and defend the Emperor. The Ruinstorm, born from the Word Bearers' treachery, presents both a physical barrier and a metaphysical trial. As the primarchs navigate the maelstrom and its corrupted worlds, each confronts visions of his own fate and grapples with the weight of past sins committed through the false Imperium Secundus. With enemies lurking in the storm and their own doubts threatening to consume them, the three brothers must overcome impossible odds—and the darkness within themselves—to fulfill their quest for redemption and reach the Throneworld.

fields.title: Old Earth
fields.synopsis: Following his resurrection on Nocturne, Vulkan embarks on a perilous journey to Terra accompanied only by his most loyal Salamander warriors, his Draaksward. Haunted by an inexplicable destiny and forced to conceal his survival from his brothers, the primarch must navigate ancient Eldar webways while evading his enemies. Meanwhile, Shadrak Meduson and the remnants of the Shattered Legions wage a desperate war against the Sons of Horus in void-space, their ranks dwindling with each brutal engagement. The Eldar seer Eldrad Ulthran orchestrates events from the shadows, pursuing his own inscrutable goals. As these narratives converge, Vulkan faces an agonizing choice between joining the crusade of vengeance or following his barely comprehensible purpose to the Imperial Palace itself. The novel weaves together multiple storylines while advancing critical plot threads toward the siege of Terra.

fields.title: The Burden of Loyalty
fields.synopsis: As the Warmaster's forces close on Terra, the servants of the Emperor fight desperately for survival on multiple fronts. This collection spans the war-ravaged Imperium, from the radiation-soaked wastelands of Mars to the Space Wolves fleets, illuminating the personal choices and costs of loyalty during the final stages of the Heresy. Two epic novellas—"The Wolf King" following Leman Russ and the Space Wolves, and "Cybernetica" revealing events on a corrupted Mars—are accompanied by six short stories exploring how individuals, factions, and entire legions navigate the impossible decisions that define the conflict. These stories showcase both grand-scale events and intimate moments of sacrifice, tracing how the war fractures everything the Imperium once held dear.

fields.title: Wolfsbane
fields.synopsis: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves Legion abandons his post at Terra to pursue Warmaster Horus before the traitor can breach the Segmentum Solar. Caught between his sacred oath and the counsel of three brother primarchs who urge caution, Russ withdraws his Legion and races toward Horus's position. Intelligence suggests the Warmaster has been fundamentally transformed, imbued with daemonic power beyond mortal comprehension. The narrative delves into Russ's psyche through flashbacks to his first meeting with Horus before the Heresy, examining their complex relationship and the manipulation that has shaped events. Alongside this introspective character study runs a parallel storyline featuring a young, gifted tech-priest named Belisarius Cawl, whose path intersects with events that will reshape the Mechanicus millennia hence. The tale balances myth-steeped introspection with visceral warfare as Russ confronts his own doubts while pursuing a foe who may be insurmountable.

fields.title: Born of Flame
fields.synopsis: This Horus Heresy anthology collects multiple stories chronicling the Salamanders Legion across the Great Crusade and the Horus Heresy. The Salamanders, whose gene-father Vulkan comes from the volcanic world of Nocturne, are known for their commitment to self-sacrifice and the preservation of human life. Through interconnected narratives, the collection explores the Legion's defining characteristics—their heroism, their capacity for betrayal, and their remarkable resilience in the face of seemingly insurmountable challenges. Each story builds toward a climactic moment that bridges the 30,000-year period and the 40,000-year era, while individual tales examine the Salamanders' philosophy of defiance and their forging of bonds amid warfare and tragedy.

fields.title: Slaves to Darkness
fields.synopsis: With Horus mortally wounded and loyalist reinforcements approaching Terra, the traitor legions must achieve unity for their final assault on the Imperium. The Warmaster lies at death's door, his spirit fractured by Leman Russ's spear, and only his equerry Maloghurst can hold the fractious Sons of Horus together. Meanwhile, Lorgar and Perturabo are tasked with controlling their daemonic brothers Fulgrim and Angron, both elevated to godhood and increasingly beyond command. As the traitor forces marshal at Ullanor for their inexorable march toward Terra, internal divisions deepen—driven by chaos itself pulling the armies apart. Through multiple viewpoints including Maloghurst, the Iron Warrior Volk, and Word Bearer Zardu Layak, the novel explores how Chaos weakens rather than strengthens its followers, examining the cracks and conflicts that threaten to unravel the Warmaster's alliance as the greatest battle the galaxy has ever known looms.

fields.title: Heralds of the Siege
fields.synopsis: As the Warmaster Horus and his fleet approach Terra, the Imperium braces for the greatest conflict in human history. This collection brings together sixteen tales from across the Horus Heresy, each offering a different perspective on the final hours before the Siege begins. Loyalist and traitor forces prepare for their fateful clash, with heroes and villains alike confronting their destinies. The stories range from grand strategic moments to intimate character studies, exploring the weight of loyalty, the cost of betrayal, and the personal sacrifices demanded by galactic war. Together, they form a tapestry of courage and desperation on the eve of Terra's darkest hour.

fields.title: Titandeath
fields.synopsis: At Beta-Garmon, Horus gathers an overwhelming war host—armies and Titans beyond count—to break the final barrier between his forces and Terra. The Imperium recognizes the strategic importance and arrays its own massive forces, including numerous Titan Legions, to hold this critical system. The narrative focuses on the escalating conflict between two Titan Legios: the loyalist Imperial Hunters and the traitorous Death Stalkers, who share a personal history beyond mere factional opposition. As these colossal God-Machines clash across the scarred world, the story captures the experience of the human crews piloting these vast engines of destruction, alongside the appearance of key Primarchs. The climactic battle at Nyrcon City—called the Titandeath for the staggering number of these divine instruments lost—determines whether Horus can proceed to Terra or face ultimate defeat. Victory here means the Emperor's dream is within reach; defeat ends the Warmaster's aspirations forever.

fields.title: The Buried Dagger
fields.synopsis: As the Horus Heresy reaches its climax, the Death Guard and their primarch Mortarion are dispatched to lead the final assault on Terra. Becalmed in the Warp and ravaged by a terrible plague, the legendary Legion faces their gravest trial yet—one that forces Mortarion to make a dark bargain that will doom his sons forever to the embrace of Nurgle. Meanwhile, on Holy Terra itself, Malcador the Sigillite and his order of exiled Knights Errant uncover a conspiracy designed to sow chaos and sedition before Horus's armies arrive. As the stories of Mortarion's tragic past and his Legion's corruption intertwine with the desperate defense preparations on Terra, the final pieces fall into place for the apocalyptic conflict to come. This novel marks a critical juncture between the Horus Heresy and the Siege of Terra, exploring the cost of loyalty, the corruption of power, and the inexorable march toward damnation.

fields.title: The Solar War
fields.synopsis: After seven years of devastating warfare across the galaxy, the Horus Heresy reaches its climax as the Warmaster's corrupted forces finally set their sights on Terra itself. Before Horus can launch his final assault on the Throneworld, he must first annihilate the defenders arrayed throughout the Sol System. Primarch Rogal Dorn, the Praetorian of Terra, deploys multiple defense lines through the outer reaches of human space, knowing that every moment of resistance buys the Emperor precious time. The narrative weaves together multiple perspectives—from the traitor forces descending upon humanity's fortress to the desperate preparations of the loyalists making their final stand. Void battles rage as fleets clash, while on distant worlds and in shadowed councils, key players from the Horus Heresy's earliest chapters emerge to shape the outcome of this monumental conflict. The story explores the deterioration of brotherhood, the corruption of ideals, and the terrible cost of civil war as the galaxy approaches its darkest hour.

fields.title: The Lost and the Damned
fields.synopsis: Horus launches his long-anticipated assault on Terra itself. With the orbital defences finally breached and a devastating warp-fueled bombardment raining down on the Throneworld, the outer redoubts of the Imperial Palace become a hellscape of attritional warfare. Billions of Imperial Guard conscripts, including the hapless Katsuhiro, are thrown into hastily constructed trenches to hold back waves of traitor forces—corrupted soldiers, cultists, and beastmen serving Horus's chosen champions. Through the poison and blood choking the frontlines, glimpses emerge of the Primarchs marshalling the loyal defence: Dorn commanding the palace fortifications, Sanguinius and the Khan coordinating the defense, while Horus and his growing army of daemon-touched warlords—Mortarion, Angron, and others—methodically grind the defenders down. The novel interweaves perspectives across the siege's opening phase, exploring both the grinding horror of the common soldier and the political machinations of the galaxy's mightiest beings as they race toward Terra's fate.

fields.title: The First Wall
fields.synopsis: The Siege of Terra enters a critical phase as the outer defensive lines fall and Rogal Dorn's Imperial Palace becomes the last bulwark against Horus's assault. To breach its walls, the Warmaster deploys Perturabo and his Iron Warriors to seize the Lion's Gate Spaceport—a strategic stronghold through which the traitors can land their most destructive weapons on Terra's surface. Thorpe weaves together multiple storylines: the grinding attrition between Dorn and Perturabo as master strategists, the investigation into the rising influence of the Lectitio Divinitatus cult within the Palace itself, and the harrowing journey of ordinary soldiers from distant Addaba as they muster to reinforce the defenders. Internal threats compound external pressure as daemonic forces take hold within Terra's own fortifications. The narrative explores themes of loyalty, sacrifice, and the hidden costs of faith amidst apocalyptic warfare, while revealing the toxic intersection of Perturabo's ambition and insecurity as he seeks to surpass his rival brother.

fields.title: Saturnine
fields.synopsis: As the siege of Terra reaches its midpoint, the traitor legions apply relentless pressure against the Imperial Palace's crumbling fortifications. Rogal Dorn, the Praetorian of Terra, commands vastly outnumbered defenders against Horus Lupercal's assault, yet the Loyalists cannot hold every position. Dorn must make impossible strategic choices, deciding which battles to sacrifice to preserve the heart of the Imperium. The narrative unfolds through multiple perspectives—from primarchs and generals down to common soldiers and remembrancers tasked with recording the siege's history. Ancient secrets emerge about the Perpetuals and the Emperor's mysterious past, challenging assumptions about the primarchs' origins and the nature of the conflict itself. As casualties mount and key figures clash in deadly confrontations, the question remains whether any single tactical stroke might turn the tide of the entire war.

fields.title: Mortis
fields.synopsis: As the Siege of Terra intensifies, the Traitor forces tighten their stranglehold on the Imperial Palace. With control of the principal space ports secured, Horus commands the immense Titan Legions to join the assault. The defenders face despair as the warp's corrupting influence spreads, torturing them even as they hold the walls. The loyalist Legio Ignatum and the Mercury Wall defenders prepare for a catastrophic clash against the towering war machines of Legio Mortis. Within the palace itself, desperate individuals scheme to counter the warp's spreading taint, while across the devastated surface of Terra, lost soldiers and refugees struggle through nightmare landscapes toward uncertain futures. The novel weaves together grand scale conflicts with intimate struggles as the Horus Heresy approaches its final stage, exploring themes of sacrifice, inevitable defeat, and the transformation of the Imperium itself.

fields.title: Warhawk
fields.synopsis: With the Imperial Palace's inner walls breached and victory seemingly within the traitors' grasp, Jaghatai Khan of the White Scars undertakes a desperate gambit. Rather than wait behind fortifications for inevitable defeat, the Khan launches the most audacious counter-offensive of the siege, seeking to retake the Lion's Gate space port from an overwhelming enemy force. Cut off from reinforcement and facing an old foe transformed beyond recognition by chaos and daemonic ascension, Khan leads his remnant Legion in a desperate assault—knowing that failure dooms not only the White Scars but Terra itself. As this pivotal strike unfolds, other threads converge within the ruins: an unwilling saint is unleashed, a dark sword rises forged from spite, and the loyalist and traitor forces collide in confrontations that will reshape the closing days of the Horus Heresy.

fields.title: Echoes of Eternity
fields.synopsis: As the walls of the Imperial Palace crumble and Terra's fate hangs by a thread, Sanguinius gathers the remnants of the Imperium's forces for one final stand at the Delphic Battlement. Angron, now a corrupted daemon primarch, leads the World Eaters in a devastating assault, while the warp itself begins poisoning the world. The Blood Angels and their scattered allies face impossible odds against overwhelming darkness. Through multiple viewpoints—from noble Space Marines to desperate servants and tech-priests—the narrative weaves together tales of defiance, personal sacrifice, and the terrible price of war. As superhuman warriors confront daemons and their own inner demons, the story explores themes of courage, kinship, and the struggle to preserve humanity amid overwhelming destruction.

added.wikipediaTitle: The End and the Death, Volume I

fields.title: The End and the Death, Volume I
fields.synopsis: After seven brutal years of civil war, the imperial palace lies in ruins and the Warmaster Horus Lupercal stands on the brink of final victory. The void-gates have crumbled, defenders have been slain, and the Emperor's grand design appears utterly shattered. Yet in this darkest hour, the Emperor himself rises to meet his beloved son in direct combat, accompanied by those he holds most loyal. Multiple story threads unfold simultaneously—vast actions of desperate hope and futile resistance play out across the dying world as thousands fight knowing they cannot win. The narrative explores the terrible cost of war when victory has already been decided, where survival becomes measured in moments and redemption seems impossible. Chaos looms triumphant while the faintest glimmer of hope remains—though whether it can alter the inexorable march toward darkness is far from certain.

added.wikipediaTitle: The End and the Death, Volume II

fields.title: The End and the Death, Volume II
fields.synopsis: At the climax of a seven-year civil war, the Warmaster Horus advances on Terra itself with intent to slay the Emperor. The primarch Sanguinius of the loyalist Blood Angels storms aboard the flagship Vengeful Spirit to confront his fallen brother and end the Heresy, defying not only Horus but the machinations of the Dark Gods themselves. As reality fractures around the siege and the final moments of this apocalyptic conflict unfold, the fate of all humanity hangs in balance. Multiple viewpoints converge on Terra as defenders fight desperately against impossible odds, cataclysmic powers awaken, and the war drives toward a conclusion that will either save mankind or consign it to eternal darkness. The outcome remains uncertain until the last breath is drawn.

added.wikipediaTitle: The End and the Death, Volume III

fields.title: The End and the Death, Volume III
fields.synopsis: The final chapter of the Horus Heresy unfolds as the Emperor confronts his fallen son Horus aboard the Vengeful Spirit. With Sanguinius slain and Terra burning under incomprehensible assault, the galaxy's fate hangs upon a catastrophic duel between deity and daemon. The Blood Angels rage in vampiric vengeance while loyalist defenders make their desperate last stand. Across the shattered homeworld, surviving legionnaires and pivotal characters converge at the apocalyptic convergence. Malcador burns upon the Golden Throne, Guilliman races across the void, and lesser figures struggle to influence an outcome most already know. The narrative weaves together intimate moments of horror and loss with galaxy-spanning conflicts, exploring themes of sacrifice, legacy, and the machinery of ruin that transforms heroic hopes into ten thousand years of darkness.

fields.title: Roboute Guilliman: Lord of Ultramar
fields.synopsis: Roboute Guilliman, the brilliant commander of the XIII Legion Ultramarines, leads his forces during the Great Crusade against an ork empire encroaching upon Ultramar. As the campaign unfolds on the war-torn world of Thoas, Guilliman grapples with profound questions about the nature of conquest and humanity's place in the galaxy. The narrative weaves together a brutal military campaign with the Primarch's internal philosophical struggles—his regret over past Imperial actions, his desire to build a better future, and his efforts to reshape his Legion's identity from mere destroyers to builders and preservers. Through personal interludes drawn from Guilliman's own treatises on war and strategy, the novel explores how even the mightiest warriors must balance ruthless pragmatism with moral conviction, ultimately forcing Guilliman to make choices that will define both his Legion and his legacy.

fields.title: Leman Russ: The Great Wolf
fields.synopsis: During the Great Crusade's height, Leman Russ leads the Space Wolves Legion alongside his brother Lion El'Jonson and the Dark Angels to bring the defiant world of Dulan into compliance. Enraged by the tyrant Durath's refusal to submit, Russ vows personal vengeance, but the cautious Lion counsels restraint. As two mighty Legions clash on an alien world, the tension between the Wolf and the Lion escalates into open rivalry, threatening to unravel their alliance. The narrative spans multiple timelines—from the Space Wolves' early days as a Chapter to the escalating siege of Dulan and beyond—revealing the complex relationship between these primarchs and the foundations of a enmity that will echo through ten thousand years of Imperial history.

added.wikipediaTitle: Magnus the Red: Master of Prospero

fields.title: Magnus the Red: Master of Prospero
fields.synopsis: During the early Great Crusade, Magnus the Red and his brother Perturabo lead their Legions in a desperate rescue mission on the dying world of Morningstar. Ravaged by catastrophic magnetic storms and geological collapse, the planet tears itself apart even as the two Primarchs coordinate evacuation efforts to save thousands of Imperial citizens. Magnus seeks forbidden knowledge within the planet's sealed archives while Perturabo commands the logistical operation with methodical precision. Their efforts grow increasingly chaotic when a cult of fanatical worshippers—the Sons of Shaitan—actively resist evacuation, viewing the planet's destruction as divine judgment. As Magnus relies upon his unparalleled psychic mastery and Perturabo reveals unexpected depths of compassion, their contrasting philosophies and approaches to this rescue shape the character arcs that will define their roles in the Heresy to come. The novel explores themes of ambition, loyalty, and the tension between knowledge-seeking and moral responsibility.

fields.title: Perturabo: The Hammer of Olympia
fields.synopsis: Perturabo, the Iron Warriors' primarch, experiences a tale of tragedy spanning two timelines. In his youth on Olympia, he emerges as a brilliant prodigy destined for greatness, yet finds himself perpetually weaponized by his adoptive father and used as a tool of war rather than celebrated as the visionary he yearned to be. Centuries later, during the Great Crusade, Perturabo and his legion struggle against the horrifying Hrud—beings that warp time and space itself—in a campaign of attrition and despair that chips away at the Iron Warriors' resolve. When word reaches him of rebellion consuming his adoptive homeworld, Perturabo abandons his impossible crusade and returns to Olympia with devastating finality. This exploration of the primarch's formative years illuminates the deep resentment and isolation that would define his descent, revealing how a child who sought only to build and be valued became a lord consumed by bitterness and the need for absolute control.

fields.title: Lorgar: Bearer of the Word
fields.synopsis: On the desert world of Colchis, a young Lorgar is discovered by the zealous priest Kor Phaeron, who recognizes in the mysterious child the potential to advance his own agenda of religious dominance. Kor Phaeron raises Lorgar as his weapon, instilling deep faith and absolute obedience through a combination of manipulation and spiritual teaching. As Lorgar matures into a prodigy of religious conviction and rhetoric, he unites Colchis's fractured faiths under the Brotherhood of Lorgar, spreading his adopted father's doctrine across the planet. Yet the primarch is haunted by cryptic visions of a greater destiny—of a supreme being called "The One" arriving from the stars. Caught between the teachings of the only father he has known and the inexplicable pull of fate, Lorgar must grapple with fundamental questions about faith, power, and his true nature, even as the foundations of his world begin to shift.

fields.title: Fulgrim: The Palatine Phoenix
fields.synopsis: Fulgrim, Primarch of the Emperor's Children, embarks on his first independent compliance mission to the world of Byzas, determined to prove himself equal to his brothers and establish his legion's place in the Great Crusade. Accompanied by only seven of his finest warriors and a diplomatic iterator, Fulgrim seeks to bring the world into compliance through cunning negotiation and political maneuvering rather than overwhelming military force. However, the Byzantine world of Byzas—with its intricate systems of aristocratic power, secret societies, and hidden agendas—proves more challenging than anticipated. As Fulgrim navigates complex courtly politics, assassination attempts, and the resistance of entrenched noble houses, he must confront the dangerous gap between his ambitions and reality. The story examines his drive for perfection and the steep price of his victories, revealing how personal insecurity and the pursuit of an impossible ideal can exact a devastating cost.

added.wikipediaTitle: Ferrus Manus: The Gorgon of Medusa

fields.title: Ferrus Manus: The Gorgon of Medusa
fields.synopsis: During the height of the Great Crusade, Ferrus Manus seeks to assert his dominance and prove his worthiness for the position of Warmaster by taking command of a stalled military campaign against the technologically advanced empire of Gardinaal. Leading a combined force of Iron Hands, Ultramarines, Thousand Sons, and Emperor's Children, Ferrus pursues a swift and overwhelming conquest to demonstrate his superiority to both his brothers and his father. Yet beneath his aggressive confidence lies a volatile temperament and deep insecurity about his standing among the primarchs. The campaign becomes an exploration of Ferrus's drive to prove himself through martial prowess, his complex relationship with his closest brother Fulgrim, and the tensions between different legion doctrines and command philosophies. As the Iron Hands assault the heavily fortified world, Ferrus's methods and character come under scrutiny from allies and enemies alike.

fields.title: Jaghatai Khan: Warhawk of Chogoris
fields.synopsis: Jaghatai Khan, the enigmatic Primarch of the White Scars, navigates a series of challenges during the height of the Great Crusade. As his Legion fights to maintain its unique warrior culture and traditions from Chogoris amid the Imperium's drive for standardization, the Khan becomes increasingly drawn into a larger ideological conflict. When the Librarius project—designed to embed psykers within the Space Marine Legions—emerges as a point of contention among the primarchs, Khan must grapple with his own knowledge of the warp's dangers and wrestle with where his true loyalty lies. Spanning multiple episodes from his discovery to the Ullanor campaign, the novel explores Khan's isolationist temperament, his relationships with key figures like Magnus and Sanguinius, and the internal contradictions between the Imperial Truth and the truths of Chogoris. The book presents a Primarch determined to forge his own path even as forces within the brotherhood seek to draw him into their schemes.

fields.title: Vulkan: Lord of Drakes
fields.synopsis: The Salamanders XVIII Legion faces a critical juncture as its warriors from two worlds—the forge-forges of Nocturne and the scattered remnants of Terra—remain fractured and untested. When a devastating Ork invasion engulfs the Taras Division, the Terran contingent finds themselves surrounded and facing annihilation. On Nocturne, the primarch Vulkan has assembled a new force, preparing to lead his sons into their first battle under his command. As these two halves of the Legion converge against overwhelming odds, Vulkan must prove himself not merely a warrior but a leader capable of forging divided soldiers into a unified whole. The clash between human ingenuity and primal destruction tests whether compassion and brotherhood can triumph against impossible odds, revealing the Salamanders' true nature as they fight for survival and identity.

fields.title: Corax: Lord of Shadows
fields.synopsis: During the Great Crusade, Primarch Corax and the Raven Guard are tasked with bringing the defiant Carinae Sodality—a civilization spanning a thousand artificial moon-cities—into compliance with the Imperium. As the campaign stalls against fierce resistance, Corax arrives determined to succeed where others have failed. However, when a dread biological weapon is unleashed at the void-city of Zenith, turning Imperial forces against one another, Corax finds himself tested in ways beyond mere military prowess. Back on his homeworld, political conflict brews as revolutionary factions seek vengeance against the ruling class. Caught between his hunger for justice and the pragmatic need for swift victory, Corax must navigate the moral ambiguities of conquest while confronting the darkness lurking within his own nature—a darkness that bears uncomfortable resemblance to his fallen brother Curze.

fields.title: Angron: Slave of Nuceria
fields.synopsis: The Primarch Angron is united with the War Hounds Legion—now renamed the World Eaters—yet bitter resentment defines his relationship with his sons. Desperate to reshape them in his image, Angron demands they adopt the Butcher's Nails, the archaic technology that ravaged his own mind on the slave-world of Nuceria. The novel interweaves two narratives: Angron's harrowing past as a gladiator under the High Riders' subjugation, and the legion's present crisis as warriors splinter between those willing to embrace the Nails for their father's approval and those who fear the transformation will destroy everything the War Hounds once valued. As a critical compliance mission deteriorates and the legion fractures, the World Eaters face an irreversible choice that will cement their descent into the blood-mad berserkers of the far future.

fields.title: Konrad Curze: The Night Haunter
fields.synopsis: In his final hours on the desolate world of Tsagualsa, the primarch Konrad Curze—the Night Haunter—awaits the assassin he has foreseen will end his life. Trapped within a shattered mind, he relives fragmented moments of his tormented past to justify his monstrous existence to a corpse-dummy fashioned in the image of his distant father. Through unreliable narration, the story traces Curze's journey from the murderous hiveworld of Nostramo, through his service to the Imperium during the Great Crusade, to his inevitable fall into darkness and madness. Haunted by prophetic visions of a future he helps create, conflicted between his desire for justice and his sadistic impulses, Curze emerges as a tragic figure—a man cursed by his own nature and the Emperor's design, struggling to find vindication in a cosmos that offers none.

added.wikipediaTitle: Lion El'Jonson: Lord of the First

fields.title: Lion El'Jonson: Lord of the First
fields.synopsis: While the Emperor marshals his greatest sons for the assault on Ullanor Prime, Lion El'Jonson draws the Dark Angels Legion away to the galaxy's farthest reaches to quell a single rebellious world in the Ghoul Stars—a graveyard of dead empires. This mysterious choice reflects the Lion's ambiguous nature: is it pride, or something far more sinister at work? The Dark Angels discover their enemy is the Khrave, a psychically-empowered species capable of seizing control of weaker minds, forcing the Legion into isolated warfare. As the psychic threat infiltrates the fleet and army, only the genetically-engineered Dark Angels can be fully trusted, compelling them to wage a desperate campaign where secrecy and severed knowledge are their only defenses. Through it all, the Lion orchestrates events from the shadows, orchestrating victory through layers of hidden planning and cutoff intelligence cells—exemplifying both his genius and the terrible isolation that defines his stewardship of the Emperor's First.

fields.title: Alpharius: Head of the Hydra
fields.synopsis: Alpharius himself narrates his enigmatic rise from mysterious origins to supreme commander of the Alpha Legion. After landing in secret on Terra, he undergoes covert training from Malcador the Sigillite, learning the arts of deception and clandestine warfare. The Emperor positions him as a hidden instrument of imperial will, tasking him with espionage and sabotage across distant worlds during the Great Crusade. Through his unreliable account, readers glimpse pivotal encounters with other Primarchs, the Custodes, and the Emperor himself—though truth and fabrication blur throughout. The narrative culminates when Alpharius discovers his long-lost twin brother Omegon across enemy lines, forcing a desperate rescue mission against the terrifying xenos known as the Slaugth. Throughout, the reader confronts the central question: can anything the serpent says be believed?

fields.title: Mortarion: The Pale King
fields.synopsis: Mortarion, newly elevated to command the Death Guard, leads a devastating compliance campaign against the Galaspar System—a world enslaved by a cruel ruling class. The primarch executes his mission with overwhelming brutality, employing toxic weapons that render the planet uninhabitable. When two fellow primarchs, Horus and Sanguinius, arrive to investigate the atrocities, they confront Mortarion over the sheer scale of his destruction. However, the Pale King refuses to justify or reconsider his methods, viewing his unflinching ruthlessness as the price of liberation and order. The narrative explores Mortarion's rigid principles, his disdain for oppression rooted in his own traumatic past, and the uncomfortable justifications he offers for wholesale slaughter in the name of Imperial justice.

fields.title: Rogal Dorn: The Emperor's Crusader
fields.synopsis: During the sixth decade of the Great Crusade, the Emperor tasks four Primarchs with a perilous mission to secure hundreds of star systems beyond the Northern Major Warp Storm—regions whose warp-churning presence blinds even the Emperor's psychic sight. Rogal Dorn leads his Imperial Fists directly into this cosmic darkness, encountering enemies and challenges unlike anything previously faced. The narrative weaves multiple perspectives: Imperial Fists warriors, a navigator struggling to guide the fleet through uncharted space, and a remembrancer documenting events. Through strategic brilliance and unwavering will, Dorn conquers the darkness in the Emperor's name. The story culminates in revealing why the Master of Mankind chose Dorn above all other Primarchs to serve as Praetorian of Terra and guardian of the Imperial Palace.

fields.title: Sanguinius: The Great Angel
fields.synopsis: A discredited remembrancer embarks on a perilous journey with the Ninth Legion during the Great Crusade, tasked with chronicling the deeds of Sanguinius, most beloved of all the primarchs. Yet the Great Angel's origins remain shrouded in mystery—his unique form an enigma, his homeworld forbidden to outsiders. As the remembrancer accompanies the Blood Angels to war against the Emperor's enemies, he uncovers far more than expected about his subject, the nature of his Legion, and the deeper truths underlying the Imperium itself. Through the lens of an outsider, the novel explores the hidden darkness and profound philosophy within the angelic facade, revealing the price civilization exacts from those who enforce it.

fields.title: Sons of the Emperor
fields.synopsis: This collection of eight interconnected short stories examines the primarchs—the nigh-immortal genetically-engineered sons of the Emperor of Mankind—through pivotal moments spanning the Great Crusade and Horus Heresy. Each narrative offers a distinct perspective on these superhuman commanders: Sanguinius bearing the moral weight of his Legion's brutal acts, Konrad Curze entangled in ancient rivalries, Vulkan encountering the Emperor himself on his homeworld, and others facing their defining challenges and transformations. The anthology spans from the nascent Imperium's golden age to its descent into civil war, revealing these demigods not as unstoppable forces but as complex beings whose humanity—or lack thereof—shaped the galaxy's darkest chapter.

fields.title: The Lords of Terra
fields.synopsis: As the Imperium expands across the galaxy under the stewardship of the Emperor and his primarch sons, darkness lurks within some of the greatest heroes. This audio drama collection presents three interconnected tales exploring the seeds of treachery in powerful leaders. One story chronicles Malcador the Sigillite, the Emperor's most trusted confidant, as he reveals closely-guarded secrets to a dying friend in her final moments while the shadow of impending war looms over Terra. The second examines Perturabo, the Lord of Iron, through a lens of cruel jealousy and ambition. The third follows Konrad Curze, the Night Haunter, in a tale that is both darkly compelling and authentically unsettling. Each narrative probes the fracturing loyalties that threaten to tear apart the very unity these leaders were meant to forge during the nascent age of the Imperium.

fields.title: Scions of the Emperor
fields.synopsis: Eight demigod primarchs—the Emperor's most powerful sons—face defining moments across the sprawling Horus Heresy timeline. From a young Ferrus Manus battling survival on a hostile world, to Rogal Dorn uncovering inexplicable deaths within Terra's sanctum as the Siege looms, these interconnected vignettes illuminate the personalities and struggles of nine different legion leaders. Each tale captures a single pivotal memory or realization: Mortarion grapples with doubt after a brutal campaign, Jaghatai Khan reminds his enemies of the cost of underestimation, and Sanguinius reveals depths beyond the battlefield. Written by some of the Black Library's finest, this collection prioritizes intimate character exploration over expansive warfare, offering quieter glimpses into the pride, hubris, loyalty, and fragile unity that bind—and ultimately tear apart—the Imperium's greatest warriors.

fields.title: Blood of the Emperor
fields.synopsis: Six interconnected tales explore the enigmatic primarchs during the tumultuous Horus Heresy, each story providing intimate glimpses into the hearts and minds of the Emperor's demigod sons. From a Cthonian warrior's rise through the stellar darkness to savage hunts across Fenris's frozen wastes, from sorcerous intrigues on Prospero to the ruthless discipline of Terra's elite guardians, these narratives illuminate the complex character of those who once commanded absolute loyalty. As brothers-in-arms fracture into warring factions—loyalist against traitor—each primarch confronts his own nature, ambitions, and destiny. The anthology reveals the pride, suffering, and terrible clarity that define these legendary figures, offering readers a multifaceted portrait of power, loyalty, and the cosmic stakes that reshape the Imperium forever.

fields.title: Valdor: Birth of the Imperium
fields.synopsis: As the wars of unification near their end, Constantin Valdor, chief of the Emperor's elite Custodian Guard, faces an unprecedented challenge. The scattered warlords of Old Terra have been conquered, but not all within the nascent Imperium accept the methods required to bring it forth. When a faction of high officials orchestrates an attempted coup to remove Valdor from power and put him on trial for his deeds, he must navigate a treacherous path of politics and dark necessity. The story unfolds through multiple perspectives—including those of the conspirators—revealing the moral compromises demanded by the birth of a galactic empire. Valdor's loyalty to the Emperor's vision is tested as he confronts the cost of progress and the secrets he must keep to preserve both Mankind's future and his own honor.

fields.title: Luther: First of the Fallen
fields.synopsis: Luther of the Dark Angels has endured ten thousand years of imprisonment, existing in stasis within the secret vaults of his own Chapter. Through a series of interrogations by successive Grand Masters, he recounts the defining moments of his life—from his emergence as a legendary knight on the savage world of Caliban, to his ascension as the Order's supreme commander, and finally his catastrophic fall into damnation. Woven between these fragmented memories of pre-Imperial glory and the grim present, Luther's account reveals a man grappling with questions of pride, loyalty, and corruption. Each tale carries lessons he desperately wishes his questioners would understand, yet the Dark Angels remain blind to the warnings embedded in his confessions. Luther claims repentance, but truth and deception coil together like serpents, and his greatest test—survival or damnation—may yet lie ahead.

fields.title: Sigismund: The Eternal Crusader
fields.synopsis: In the closing days of the Great Crusade, Remembrancer Solomon Voss seeks an audience with Sigismund, First Captain of the Imperial Fists, to understand why this legendary champion believes war will never end despite the Imperium's apparent triumph. Through a series of interviews, Sigismund reveals his extraordinary journey from a mortal child to the greatest warrior of the Legions—from his early years and first battles through his induction into the Space Marines, his formative oaths, and his climactic duels with champions from other Legions. The narrative explores the brutal indoctrination process, his relationships with his brothers, and the philosophical convictions that would eventually found the Black Templars. Readers witness Sigismund's transformation and gain insight into the costs of becoming post-human, revealing a warrior shaped by tragedy and duty into an eternal crusader.

fields.title: Eidolon: The Auric Hammer
fields.synopsis: Lord Commander Eidolon of the Emperor's Children, a legendary traitor legion commander, finds himself tormented by daemonic manifestations aboard his vessel. Drawn inexorably toward the world of Tatricala by whispered warp-truths, Eidolon confronts the fragmented remnants of his own psyche while pursued by the insidious influence of Chaos itself. As he traverses this haunted world, the ghosts of his past—particularly his fraught history with Primarch Fulgrim—threaten to consume him entirely. The novel traces Eidolon's inner descent into madness and corruption as he races toward Terra and the culmination of the Horus Heresy, forcing him to choose between surrendering utterly to Chaos damnation or clawing back some semblance of his fractured identity.

fields.title: Promethean Sun
fields.synopsis: During the Great Crusade, the Imperium discovers a feral world enthralled by the Eldar and dispatches three Imperial Legions to liberate it. The Iron Hands of Ferrus Manus, the Death Guard under Mortarion, and the Salamanders of Vulkan engage the alien occupiers in bitter jungle warfare against exotic reptilian creatures and eldritch sorcery. As the campaign unfolds, the narrative shifts between present-day battles and flashbacks revealing Vulkan's early reunification with the Emperor and his transformation into a warrior-primarch who aspires toward nobility beyond mere conquest. Ultimately, Vulkan himself must intervene to overcome the Eldar's sinister designs, forcing Imperial forces to confront difficult moral questions about the price of liberation and the character of their leaders.
